what: Step 1: Defining file path and processing information
title: Reading & processing .TIF drone files
text: First thing define location .TIF file want process. kind files usually pretty big exceed Git Hub’s maximum file size limit recommend keeping separate data folder. case, file path: addition, also define 3 inputs function. First Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM) zone drone flight took place. UTM coordinate system divides world 60 north-south zones, 6 degrees longitude wide. calculate UTM zone flight took place, can use rough longitude flight took place apply formula: \[ UTM_{zone} = \lfloor \frac{Longitude + 180}{6} \rfloor + 1 \] case, average longitude flight used example -119.45 UTM zone : Next need define hemisphere flight took place either north south. case, example flight took place northern hemisphere: Lastly, need define number decimal digits coordinate. Using decimal coordinate system (.e., -119.45 instead coordinate systems like DMS 119° 27’ 0” W highly discourage) decimal degree convey certain distance. distances differ longitude latitude degrees become smaller higher latitudes. part documentation package included data set (spatial_resolution_lat_long.csv) visualize pattern. plot generated data express area rectangle sides (representing longitude latitude) specific number decimal digits (.e., 1, 0.1, 0.001 etc.) different latitudes.  seen , recommend spatial resolution 5 decimal places since, depending latitude, convey area smaller 1 m^2 relevant scale vast majority terrestrial ectotherms:
code: filepath <- "C:/Users/ggarc/OneDrive/projects/throne_data/test_flight.tif" utm_zone = floor((-119.45 + 180) / 6) + 1 utm_zone ## [1] 11 hemisphere <- "north" ## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. ## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. decimal_places <- 5

what: Step 2: Reading .TIF file and transform into a tibble object
title: Reading & processing .TIF drone files
text: Using file path defined earlier, can use raster function package name order read file R environment: file load Formal class RasterLayer can actually plotted using base R:  order easy work data, can use rasterToPoints function raster package transform raster object data frame. resulting data frame 3 columns. First x y, equivalent longitude latitude (although different units, ) column called layer equivalent temperature drone captured degrees Celsius. number rows data frame heavily dependent resolution drone’s camera. example file obtained using DRONE CAMERA bit overkill. 10th observations worked just fine. moving next steps, important note majority observations layer column 0. temperature recorded drone exactly 0 drone record data. 0 observations removed reduces size final data frame substantially.
